items.title: LIVE: 2 people killed and 8 others injured in shooting at Brown University
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live from Brown University in Rhode Island. At least 2 people have been killed and 8 others were injured in a shooting in the area of Brown University on Saturday, Providence Mayor Brett Smiley said.

Credit: WCVB and WJAR</article>

items.title: Scene at Brown University after at least 2 killed and more hurt in shooting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 2 people have been killed and several others injured in a shooting at Brown University, a law enforcement source told the Associated Press. (AP video shot by: Kimberlee Kruesi).

#brownuniversity #news

items.title: Trump says 'we will retaliate' after 3 Americans killed in Syria attack
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump says “we will retaliate” after two U.S. service members and one American civilian were killed in an attack in Syria that the United States blames on the Islamic State group.

#trump #syria

items.title: Chile chooses between arch-conservative and communist in presidential runoff
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Chileans on Sunday are set to elect their next president in a high-stakes runoff between an arch-conservative and a communist. (AP Video by Mauricio Cuevas).

#chile 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Belarus releases opposition figures as US lifts sanctions
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Belarusian authorities have released Nobel Peace Prize laureate Ales Bialiatski and key opposition figure Maria Kolesnikova from prison, Pavel Sapelka, human rights advocate with the Viasna rights group, confirmed to the AP. Their release comes as authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko seeks to improve relations with Washington. 

#belarus 
Photo credit: Press Service Of Coordination Headquarters for the Treatment of Prisoners of War

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Wolf from a French supermarket ad is moving viewers worldwide
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A French supermarket’s Christmas advert is doing something most global brands only dream of: connecting deeply with millions of people around the world, without a single frame of generative AI.

Photo credit: Romance/Illogic Studios (Wizz)/Nadège Loiseau/Wibke Rauers

items.title: Dick Van Dyke turns 100 years old
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Dick Van Dyke is 100 years old. Honored by the Kennedy Center in 2021, the actor and Broadway star quipped: "How I got here, I don't know. And I'm not going to ask."
#DickvanDyke #ByeByeBirdie #TheDickVanDykeShow #MaryPoppins #ChittyChittyBangBang</article>

items.title: Online rise of Eastern Orthodoxy tests its clergy
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Many new converts are discovering Eastern Orthodoxy online. But church leaders say the ancient Christian faith is meant to be lived in person, through worship, ritual and community.</article>

items.title: Kyle Van Noy on Ravens' controversial loss vs. Steelers and facing Joe Burrow's Bengals
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ravens Pro Bowl linebacker Kyle Van Noy talks about Baltimore’s loss vs. Steelers and their upcoming game against Joe Burrow's Bengals, compares Lamar Jackson to Tom Brady and discusses being nominated for the Art Rooney sportsmanship award. Plus Philip Rivers comes out of retirement to join the Colts.

#ravens #baltimoreravens</article>

items.title: Thailand villagers stay behind to feed animals and guard homes during clashes with Cambodia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>As clashes along the Thai-Cambodian border send hundreds of thousands of Thai villagers to flee their homes, teams of volunteers have stayed behind to look after their communities' livestock and pets. (AP Video by Tian Macleod Ji).

#thailand #cambodia 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Kim Jong Un welcomes back North Korean soldiers returning from Russia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Video from North Korea state broadcaster KRT showed a big ceremony on Friday for soldiers returning from fighting in Russia, in attendance North Korean leader Kim Jong Un hugging the soldiers, as well as laying a white flower in front of portraits of the nine who died in combat.

#kimjongun #northkorea 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Meet the Indigenous women behind Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum's looks
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>These talented Indigenous artisans create colorful embroidered dresses and blouses, that have become a closet staple for Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum and earned her a spot on the New York Times “Most Stylish” list. (AP Video by Fernanda Pesce)

items.title: Aerial video shows severe flooding in Washington state
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Aerial footage from Thursday December 11th, shows deputies from the King County Sheriff's Office Marine Rescue Dive Unit assisting residents from Duvall, Washington as many in the region experience flooding this week. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Trump signs bill honoring 'Miracle on Ice' USA hockey team
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump signed legislation authorizing a coin in honor of the USA hockey team’s gold medal victory over the Soviet Union at the 1980 Games in Lake Placid, New York.

items.title: Costa Rica President Rodrigo Chaves tours El Salvador mega-prison
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Costa Rican President Rodrigo Chaves toured a sprawling mega-prison for alleged gang members on Friday in Tecoluca, El Salvador, during an official visit centered on security cooperation with President Nayib Bukele.
